SEARCH
Package project2_root.database

Class SQLServerPOSDatabase

java.lang.Object
project2_root.database.SQLServerPOSDatabase
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable, POSDatabase
public class SQLServerPOSDatabase extends Object implements POSDatabase, AutoCloseable
PostgreSQL implementation of POSDatabase.

All public methods follow a consistent pattern: they delegate to a private attempt*() method that throws SQLException, catch any exception at the public boundary, log it, and return a safe fallback value. Transactional methods disable auto-commit, roll back on failure, and restore auto-commit in a finally block.

    Description

    Inserts a new employee record.
    int

    Inserts a new ingredient with zero starting stock and returns the generated ID.
    void
    addItemToOrder(int orderId, OrderItem item)
    Inserts a line item into the OrderItem table and increments the parent order's running cost by the item's cost.

    Inserts a new menu item with the given name, cost, and ingredient associations.

    Returns whether today's Z-report can still be generated.

    Closes the underlying JDBC connection.
    void

    Marks the given restock order as confirmed and increments ingredient stock levels by the ordered quantities.

    connect(String url, String user, String password)
    Opens a JDBC connection to the given PostgreSQL server.
    int
    createOrder(int employeeId)
    Inserts a new order row for the given employee and returns the generated order ID.
    void
    decrementIngredientsForItem(OrderItem item)
    Decrements each ingredient's servings_available by the quantity used to make the given order item.
    ZReportSummary
    finalizeCurrentZReport()
    Persists the current day's Z-report summary and returns the generated record.
    Collection<Ingredient>
    getAllIngredients()
    Returns all ingredients from the ingredient table, ordered by name.
    Collection<Ingredient>
    getCriticalIngredients(int weeksToCover)
    Returns all ingredients whose current stock falls below the threshold needed to cover the given number of weeks of projected demand, along with a computed recommended restock quantity for each.
    Collection<Employee>
    getEmployees()
    Returns all employees from the employee table, ordered by last name ascending.
    List<HourlySalesRow>
    getHourlySalesToday()
    Returns hourly sales buckets since the last generated Z-report.
    List<ItemIngredient>
    getIngredientsForMenuItem(int itemId)
    Returns all ingredient associations for a given menu item, including each ingredient's per-unit cost.
    List<IngredientStock>
    getIngredientStock()
    Returns a snapshot of all ingredient stock levels, ordered by servings_available ascending so the lowest-stock items appear first.
    Collection<IngredientUsageRow>
    getIngredientUsage(LocalDate start, LocalDate end)
    Returns total ingredient servings consumed by completed orders whose creation date falls within [start, end] inclusive, ordered by servings used descending.
    MenuItem
    getMenuItemById(int itemId)
    Returns a single menu item by ID.
    Collection<MenuItem>
    getMenuItems()
    Returns all menu items from the item table, ordered by name.
    int
    getOrderCountToday()
    Returns the number of orders placed since the last generated Z-report.
    Collection<OrderItem>
    getOrderItems(int orderId)
    Returns all line items belonging to the given order, with item names joined from the item table.
    Collection<RestockOrder>
    getRestockOrders()
    Returns all restock orders with their line items, ordered by date descending.
    List<TimeBucketSales>
    getSalesBetween(LocalDate startDate, LocalDate endDate, boolean weekly)
    Returns aggregated sales totals bucketed by week or month for the given date range.
    List<ItemSalesRow>
    getSalesByItemBetween(LocalDate startDate, LocalDate endDate)
    Returns item-level sales totals (quantity sold and revenue) for all orders whose creation date falls within the given range, ordered by revenue descending.
    List<ItemSalesRow>
    getSalesByItemToday()
    Returns item-level sales totals since the last generated Z-report, ordered by revenue descending.
    double
    getTotalSalesToday()
    Returns total sales since the last generated Z-report.
    List<ZReportSummary>
    getZReportHistory()
    Returns historical Z-reports in reverse chronological order.

    Deletes a single order item by ID and subtracts its cost from the parent order's running total.

    Creates a new pending restock order with the given ingredient quantities.

  • Constructor Details

    • SQLServerPOSDatabase

      public SQLServerPOSDatabase()

  • Method Details

    • connect

      public void connect(String url, String user, String password)
      Opens a JDBC connection to the given PostgreSQL server.
      Parameters:
      url - the JDBC connection URL including host and database name
      user - the database username
      password - the database password

    • close

      public void close()
      Closes the underlying JDBC connection. Should be called when the application shuts down.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable

    • validateLogin

      public Optional<Employee> validateLogin(int employeeId, String password)
      Validates login credentials against the employee table.
      Specified by:
      validateLogin in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the employee ID to authenticate
      password - the password to verify
      Returns:
      the matching Employee when authentication succeeds, or empty otherwise

    • getMenuItems

      public Collection<MenuItem> getMenuItems()
      Returns all menu items from the item table, ordered by name. Ingredient associations are not loaded here; an empty map is used instead.
      Specified by:
      getMenuItems in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      a collection of all MenuItem records, or an empty list on error

    • createOrder

      public int createOrder(int employeeId)
      Inserts a new order row for the given employee and returns the generated order ID. The order is created with a cost of zero; item costs are accumulated as items are added via addItemToOrder(int, project2_root.database.entities.OrderItem).
      Specified by:
      createOrder in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the ID of the employee placing the order
      Returns:
      the generated order ID, or -1 on failure

    • addItemToOrder

      public void addItemToOrder(int orderId, OrderItem item)
      Inserts a line item into the OrderItem table and increments the parent order's running cost by the item's cost.
      Specified by:
      addItemToOrder in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      orderId - the ID of the order to add the item to
      item - the order item to insert, including quantity and customizations

    • getRestockOrders

      public Collection<RestockOrder> getRestockOrders()
      Returns all restock orders with their line items, ordered by date descending. Results are assembled by grouping flat join rows by order ID.
      Specified by:
      getRestockOrders in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      a collection of RestockOrder records, or an empty list on error

    • getCriticalIngredients

      public Collection<Ingredient> getCriticalIngredients(int weeksToCover)
      Returns all ingredients whose current stock falls below the threshold needed to cover the given number of weeks of projected demand, along with a computed recommended restock quantity for each.
      Specified by:
      getCriticalIngredients in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      weeksToCover - the number of weeks of demand the stock must cover
      Returns:
      a collection of critical Ingredient records ordered by stock level ascending, or an empty list on error

    • getAllIngredients

      public Collection<Ingredient> getAllIngredients()
      Returns all ingredients from the ingredient table, ordered by name. recommendedRestockQty is set to zero since it is not computed here.
      Specified by:
      getAllIngredients in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      a collection of all Ingredient records, or an empty list on error

    • submitRestockOrder

      public void submitRestockOrder(Map<Integer,Integer> ingredientQuantities)
      Creates a new pending restock order with the given ingredient quantities. The unit cost for each line item is read directly from the ingredient table at the time of insertion.
      Specified by:
      submitRestockOrder in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      ingredientQuantities - a map of ingredient ID to ordered quantity; should contain only non-zero entries

    • confirmOrder

      public void confirmOrder(int orderId)
      Marks the given restock order as confirmed and increments ingredient stock levels by the ordered quantities. Both updates are executed in a single transaction.
      Specified by:
      confirmOrder in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      orderId - the ID of the restock order to confirm

    • saveMenuItem

      public void saveMenuItem(int id, String name, double cost, Map<Integer,Integer> ingredients)
      Updates an existing menu item's name and cost, then replaces all of its ingredient associations with the provided map.
      Specified by:
      saveMenuItem in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the menu item to update
      name - the updated item name
      cost - the updated price
      ingredients - a map of ingredient ID to quantity; zero-quantity entries should be filtered out before calling

    • addMenuItem

      public void addMenuItem(String name, double cost, Map<Integer,Integer> ingredients)
      Inserts a new menu item with the given name, cost, and ingredient associations.
      Specified by:
      addMenuItem in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the new item
      cost - the price of the new item
      ingredients - a map of ingredient ID to quantity

    • getEmployees

      public Collection<Employee> getEmployees()
      Returns all employees from the employee table, ordered by last name ascending.
      Specified by:
      getEmployees in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      a collection of all Employee records, or an empty list on error

    • saveEmployee

      public void saveEmployee(int employeeId, String firstName, String lastName, boolean isManager)
      Updates an existing employee's name and manager status.
      Specified by:
      saveEmployee in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the ID of the employee to update
      firstName - the updated first name
      lastName - the updated last name
      isManager - whether the employee should have manager privileges

    • getOrderItems

      public Collection<OrderItem> getOrderItems(int orderId)
      Returns all line items belonging to the given order, with item names joined from the item table.
      Specified by:
      getOrderItems in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      orderId - the ID of the order whose items to retrieve
      Returns:
      a collection of OrderItem records, or an empty list on error

    • removeOrderItem

      public void removeOrderItem(int orderItemId)
      Deletes a single order item by ID and subtracts its cost from the parent order's running total.
      Specified by:
      removeOrderItem in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      orderItemId - the ID of the order item to remove

    • completeOrder

      public void completeOrder(int orderId)
      No-op. Order completion is handled in memory and does not require a database update in the current implementation.
      Specified by:
      completeOrder in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      orderId - the ID of the order being completed

    • getIngredientStock

      public List<IngredientStock> getIngredientStock()
      Returns a snapshot of all ingredient stock levels, ordered by servings_available ascending so the lowest-stock items appear first.
      Specified by:
      getIngredientStock in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      a list of IngredientStock records, or an empty list on error

    • addEmployee

      public void addEmployee(String firstName, String lastName, boolean isManager)
      Inserts a new employee record. The employee ID is assigned by the database.
      Specified by:
      addEmployee in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      firstName - the new employee's first name
      lastName - the new employee's last name
      isManager - whether the new employee should have manager privileges

    • getSalesBetween

      public List<TimeBucketSales> getSalesBetween(LocalDate startDate, LocalDate endDate, boolean weekly)
      Returns aggregated sales totals bucketed by week or month for the given date range. Uses DATE_TRUNC to align bucket boundaries and an exclusive upper bound to match the inclusive end date.
      Specified by:
      getSalesBetween in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      startDate - the inclusive start of the date range
      endDate - the inclusive end of the date range
      weekly - true to bucket by week, false to bucket by month
      Returns:
      a list of TimeBucketSales rows ordered by bucket start date, or an empty list on error

    • getSalesByItemBetween

      public List<ItemSalesRow> getSalesByItemBetween(LocalDate startDate, LocalDate endDate)
      Returns item-level sales totals (quantity sold and revenue) for all orders whose creation date falls within the given range, ordered by revenue descending.
      Specified by:
      getSalesByItemBetween in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      startDate - the inclusive start of the date range
      endDate - the inclusive end of the date range
      Returns:
      a list of ItemSalesRow records, or an empty list on error

    • addIngredient

      public int addIngredient(String name, double addCost)
      Inserts a new ingredient with zero starting stock and returns the generated ID.
      Specified by:
      addIngredient in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      name - the ingredient name
      addCost - the per-unit restock cost
      Returns:
      the generated ingredient ID, or -1 on failure

    • getMenuItemById

      public MenuItem getMenuItemById(int itemId)
      Returns a single menu item by ID. Ingredient associations are not loaded; an empty map is used instead.
      Specified by:
      getMenuItemById in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      itemId - the ID of the menu item to retrieve
      Returns:
      the matching MenuItem, or null if not found or on error

    • decrementIngredientsForItem

      public void decrementIngredientsForItem(OrderItem item)
      Decrements each ingredient's servings_available by the quantity used to make the given order item. The decrement per ingredient is orderItem.quantity * itemingredient.quantity.
      Specified by:
      decrementIngredientsForItem in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      item - the order item whose ingredient consumption should be applied

    • getOrderCountToday

      public int getOrderCountToday()
      Returns the number of orders placed since the last generated Z-report.
      Specified by:
      getOrderCountToday in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      the current order count for the active business day

    • getTotalSalesToday

      public double getTotalSalesToday()
      Returns total sales since the last generated Z-report.
      Specified by:
      getTotalSalesToday in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      the current sales total for the active business day

    • getHourlySalesToday

      public List<HourlySalesRow> getHourlySalesToday()
      Returns hourly sales buckets since the last generated Z-report.
      Specified by:
      getHourlySalesToday in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      hourly sales rows for the active business day, or an empty list on error

    • getSalesByItemToday

      public List<ItemSalesRow> getSalesByItemToday()
      Returns item-level sales totals since the last generated Z-report, ordered by revenue descending.
      Specified by:
      getSalesByItemToday in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      item sales rows for the active business day, or an empty list on error

    • canGenerateZReport

      public boolean canGenerateZReport()
      Returns whether today's Z-report can still be generated.
      Specified by:
      canGenerateZReport in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      true when no Z-report has been recorded for the current date

    • finalizeCurrentZReport

      public ZReportSummary finalizeCurrentZReport()
      Persists the current day's Z-report summary and returns the generated record.
      Specified by:
      finalizeCurrentZReport in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      the stored ZReportSummary, or a fallback summary when insertion fails

    • getZReportHistory

      public List<ZReportSummary> getZReportHistory()
      Returns historical Z-reports in reverse chronological order.
      Specified by:
      getZReportHistory in interface POSDatabase
      Returns:
      previously generated ZReportSummary records, or an empty list on error

    • getIngredientUsage

      public Collection<IngredientUsageRow> getIngredientUsage(LocalDate start, LocalDate end)
      Returns total ingredient servings consumed by completed orders whose creation date falls within [start, end] inclusive, ordered by servings used descending.
      Specified by:
      getIngredientUsage in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      start - the inclusive start date of the query window
      end - the inclusive end date of the query window
      Returns:
      a collection of IngredientUsageRow records, or an empty list on error

    • getIngredientsForMenuItem

      public List<ItemIngredient> getIngredientsForMenuItem(int itemId)
      Returns all ingredient associations for a given menu item, including each ingredient's per-unit cost.
      Specified by:
      getIngredientsForMenuItem in interface POSDatabase
      Parameters:
      itemId - the ID of the menu item whose ingredients to retrieve
      Returns:
      a list of ItemIngredient records, one per ingredient
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - wrapping SQLException if the query fails
